_researchNotes: Happy-path WeddingWire lead notification. Subject shape is OFFICIAL: WeddingWire Vendor Support article 115003720143 ('Responding to a Lead from your WeddingWire Email Inbox', updated 2022-02-04, fetched via the Zendesk Help Center API at vendorsupport.weddingwire.com/api/v2/help_center/en-us/articles/115003720143.json) quotes: "An example of a new lead subject line is: 'New lead from John Doe'". Sender: article 115003741366 ('Not Receiving WeddingWire Emails') lists the official whitelist 'pros@weddingwire.com, promotions@weddingwire.com, email@emailsweddingwire.com' — pros@weddingwire.com is the vendor-notification address. Unlike GigSalad, WeddingWire EXPOSES client contact info: article 115003720143 says vendors can 'Respond directly to the client by clicking the reply button in your email interface' and the response 'will always be sent to the client's email address' (so the lead email carries the couple's address, here mirrored in Reply-To), plus a blue 'within your account' link at the bottom. Field set per article 360027979832 ('View Details of an Inquiry'): name, event date, phone number, email address; WeddingPro docs add wedding date + guest count as the couple-provided details. Exact body label wording is not published verbatim by WeddingWire — labels here (Wedding Date / Number of Guests / Phone / Email / Message) follow the couple-facing storefront contact form fields; treat label variants as a real possibility.
subject: New lead from Jessica Miller
textBody:
Hi Demo DJ Co.,

You have a new lead! Reply quickly to connect with this couple.

Jessica Miller

Wedding Date: Saturday, October 17, 2026
Number of Guests: 150
Phone: (312) 555-0147
Email: jessica.miller84@example.com

Message:
Hi! We're getting married next October at the Riverwalk Pavilion and love your reviews. Are you still available on our date, and could you send over your packages and pricing?

Reply directly to this email to respond, or reply within your account.

The WeddingWire Team
